inline_review:
commit 3b3de040ca34238b010d9771c39c74652418a47d
Author: Shivam Kalra <shivamkalra98@zohomail.in>

lib/test_vmalloc: add vrealloc test case

This commit introduces a new test case to exercise the vrealloc() shrink
and in-place grow paths within the test_vmalloc suite.

> diff --git a/lib/test_vmalloc.c b/lib/test_vmalloc.c
> index 876c72c18a0c..ce2b2777a785 100644
> --- a/lib/test_vmalloc.c
> +++ b/lib/test_vmalloc.c

[ ... ]

> @@ -421,6 +422,56 @@ vm_map_ram_test(void)
> +static int vrealloc_test(void)
> +{
> +	void *ptr;
> +	int i;
> +
> +	for (i = 0; i < test_loop_count; i++) {
> +		ptr = vmalloc(PAGE_SIZE);
> +		if (!ptr)
> +			return -1;
> +
> +		*((__u8 *)ptr) = 'a';
> +
> +		/* Grow: beyond allocated pages, triggers full realloc. */
> +		ptr = vrealloc(ptr, 4 * PAGE_SIZE, GFP_KERNEL);

If vrealloc() fails, it returns NULL but leaves the original memory
allocation intact. By assigning the result directly back to ptr, do we
lose the reference to the original memory and leak it?

> +		if (!ptr)
> +			return -1;
> +
> +		if (*((__u8 *)ptr) != 'a')
> +			return -1;

If this data integrity check fails, the function returns -1 directly.
Should this call vfree(ptr) before returning to avoid leaking the
allocation?

[ ... ]
output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"Memory leak on error paths in vrealloc_test","severity":"Medium","severity_explanation":"1. `vrealloc()` behaves like standard `realloc()`: if reallocation fails, it returns `NULL` but leaves the original memory block intact. The test does `ptr = vrealloc(ptr, ...)` which overwrites `ptr` with `NULL` on failure, losing the reference to the originally allocated memory and causing a leak.
2. If any of the data integrity checks fail (e.g., `if (*((__u8 *)ptr) != 'a')`), the function returns `-1` without explicitly calling `vfree(ptr)` to clean up the existing allocation.
These issues lead to kernel memory leaks on test failure paths. Because this occurs on cold paths in test code, the severity is evaluated as Medium."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit 3b3de040ca34238b010d9771c39c74652418a47d
